hero.label: Who We Are
hero.title: About RAPP
hero.subtitle: We are Rwandans Allied for Peace and Progress — a movement rooted in hope, driven by community, and committed to lasting change.

story.label: Our Story
story.title: Born from Rwanda's Spirit of Resilience
paragraphs: RAPP was founded in 2014 by a group of Rwandan peacebuilders, academics, and community leaders who believed that Rwanda's remarkable post-genocide transformation held lessons for the world — and that more could be done to sustain and deepen that transformation.

Starting with a single reconciliation programme in Kigali, we have grown into a nationwide organization with programmes spanning education, health, agriculture, women's empowerment, and environmental sustainability.

Our approach is simple: listen deeply, act boldly, and measure relentlessly. We believe that peace and progress are not abstract ideals — they are daily choices, community decisions, and collective commitments.

values.title: Peace First
values.desc: We believe dialogue and reconciliation are the foundation of all sustainable progress.

values.title: Community-Led
values.desc: Every programme is designed with — not for — the communities we serve.

values.title: Pan-African Spirit
values.desc: We draw on Rwanda's rich heritage while embracing our shared African identity.

values.title: Accountability
values.desc: We hold ourselves to the highest standards of transparency and results-based management.

values.title: Sustainability
values.desc: We build programmes that communities can own and sustain long after our involvement.

values.title: Equity & Inclusion
values.desc: We prioritize the most marginalized — women, youth, and rural communities.

milestones.event: RAPP founded in Kigali by a coalition of peacebuilders and development practitioners.
Launched first reconciliation dialogue programme, reaching 20 communities.
Received first international grant from the EU to expand to the Northern Province.
Scaled Youth Leadership Academy to three provinces, training 300+ youth annually.
Celebrated impacting over 40,000 lives and partnering with 25+ organizations.
Launched the Reforestation Rwanda campaign with a goal of 1 million trees by 2027.

team.name: Dr. Amina Uwimana
team.bio: A peacebuilding expert with 20+ years in conflict resolution and community development across East Africa.

team.name: Jean-Pierre Habimana
team.bio: An engineer-turned-development practitioner who oversees all field programmes and ensures measurable impact.

team.name: Grace Mukamana
team.bio: Former UN staff with deep expertise in building strategic alliances with international and local organizations.

team.name: Eric Nshimiyimana
team.bio: A storyteller who brings RAPP's mission to life through compelling narratives and digital outreach.

team.name: Claudine Umubyeyi
team.bio: CPA-certified accountant ensuring financial integrity and transparent stewardship of donor resources.

team.name: Patrick Nkurunziza
team.bio: Oversees community programmes across the Northern Province with a focus on youth and reconciliation.

team.name: Vestine Mukamusoni
team.bio: Designs and runs cooperative and micro-finance programmes that lift women out of poverty sustainably.

team.name: Innocent Bizimana
team.bio: Builds the data systems and evaluation frameworks that keep RAPP accountable to the communities we serve.

team.name: Alphonsine Nyiramana
team.bio: Manages the CHW network across the Eastern Province, training frontline health workers and tracking outcomes.
